steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word "However" signals that new evidence contradicts the original hypothesis. The climate shift occurred too early to have caused the extinction around 200,000 years ago, so paleontologists must respond to this contradiction by changing their view.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'revise': means to reconsider and change a view based on new information — exactly what scientists must do when a hypothesis is contradicted by evidence. ✓
  • Choice B — 'interpret': means to analyze or explain meaning; it doesn't capture the act of changing a hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice C — 'recollect': means to remember something; irrelevant to updating a scientific position. ✗
  • Choice D — 'introduce': means to present something new; the hypothesis already exists, so it can't be introduced again. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
